mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand recovery that lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals resilient manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings stability, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of observations during tightening cycles, or the predictive IC drops below zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 330.0 reflects entrenched inflation that erodes real purchasing power and forces aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 70 percent of high-inflation observations, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for lagged market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects underlying economic expansion that translates into higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of positive GDP growth quarters, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contracting manufacturing sector that reduces corporate revenue expectations and triggers negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances, or the decline threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods in historical backtests.
